rulings.Case Title: JIM KEHOE VS. MICHAEL O'BRIEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To File X-Complt
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 13, 2013, Line 11, DEFENDANTs MICHAEL O'BRIEN and JOE O'BRIEN's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Cross-Complaint. Defendants' motion for leave to file a cross-complaint is granted. Defendants have established that the proposed cross-complaint is compulsory, as it arises from the same series of transactions or occurrences as the claims brought against them in Plaintiffs' complaint. Defendants did not act in bad faith by seeking leave at this stage in the lawsuit; they received the DMV Investigation file on July 22, 2013, and filed the present motion on August 28, 2013. (Declaration of Patrick In Support of Defendants' Motion for Leave to File Cross-Complaint, 2-5.) Plaintiffs have not produced any evidence to support their argument that Defendants knew of the DMV Investigation File, and its findings, at the time Defendants filed their answer. Because the current trial date is September 30, 2013, it must be continued to allow plaintiff to respond to the proposed cross-complaint. The trial date is continued to January 21, 2013 at 9:30 a.m. The discovery cut-off will be based on the new trial date. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. rulings.Case Title: CHRISTIAN K. JENSEN VS. VIRGIN ATLANTIC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Dismiss
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 13, 2013, Line 12, DEFENDANT VIRGIN ATLANTIC AIRWAYS, LTD's Motion To Dismiss. The motion to dismiss pursuant to CCP §581(f)(2) is denied. CCP §472b states in pertinent part: "When a demurrer to any pleading is sustained or overruled, and time to amend or answer is given, the time so given runs from the service of notice of the decision or order, unless the notice is waived in open court, and the waiver entered in the minutes." Defendant has not established that it ever provided service of notice of the Court's order filed June 24, 2013. Plaintiff was not present in the courtroom when the order was signed on June 24, 2013. Further, even if notice had been provided by hand on June 24, 2013, filing an amended complaint on July 5, 2013 is timely. CCP §12 states that "The time in which any act provided by law is to be done is computed by excluding the first day and including the last, unless the last day is a holiday, and then it is also excluded." By this calculation, June 25 was the first day and July 4, 2013 was the last day and because it was a holiday, July 5, 2013 was the last day to file. The motion is thus completely without merit. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL RAYBIN VS. RAYMOND MICHAEL DELAGNES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Seal Records Filed Conditionally Under Seal
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 13, 2013, Line 14, PLAINTIFF PAUL RAYBIN's Motion To Seal Records Filed Conditionally Under Seal. Granted, for good cause shown. No opposition filed. Moving party shall prepare a proposed order which includes the express factual findings required by CRC 2.551(d). The Court finds that there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record; the overriding interest of personal financial privacy supports sealing the record; a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest in moving party's financial privacy will be prejudiced if the records are not sealed; the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored, and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. The Court assumes that the moving party wishes to seal portions of the Reply Separate Statement (conditionally filed under seal) which are identical to the portions of the Separate Statement for which sealing is sought. If that is the case, the proposed order to be drafted by the moving party must specifically refer to that document. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: SYDNEY SAIDYAN VS. AMERICAN LASER CENTERS OF CALIFORNIA LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 13, 2013, Line 15, DEFENDANTs AMERICAN LASER CENTERS OF CALIFORNIA LLC, AMERICAN LASER SKINCARE LLC and BULLUS ALC OF CALIFORNIA, LLC's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Denied. The motion to strike is denied as to the prayer for injunctive relief. The complaint alleges that the conduct is likely to repeat and the Court takes that allegation as true for purposes of this motion. The Court denies the motion to strike as to the prayer for prejudgment interest under CC 3288. The Court denies the motion to strike as to the prayer for attorney's fees; it is a factual issue that cannot yet be determined. rulings.Case Title: SYDNEY SAIDYAN VS. AMERICAN LASER CENTERS OF CALIFORNIA LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 13, 2013, Line 15, DEFENDANT AMERICAN LASER CENTERS OF CALIFORNIA LLC, AMERICAN LASER SKINCARE LLC and BULLUS ALC OF CALIFORNIA, LL's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled in its entirety. Defendant fails to present clear authority that the negligence cause of action subsumes the negligent misrepresentation and lack of informed consent causes of action. The negligent misrepresentation, intentional misrepresentation, and UCL causes of action are alleged with sufficient particularity. The complaint alleges outrageous conduct to support the intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action. rulings.Case Title: MARK MCGOVERN VS. PATRICK DANIEL POWER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Stay Action
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 13, 2013, Line 18, DEFENDANT PATRICK POWER's Amended Motion To Stay Action. The motion to stay is granted. It is undisputed that defendant is exposed to criminal prosecution arising from the same facts as alleged in the civil case as an intentional tort. Considering the circumstances of this case where the entire subject matter of the civil case is the same as the pending criminal investigation, the best solution to fairly balance the interests of the parties and the judicial system is to stay the action pending the expiration of the statute of limitations or the resolution of the criminal prosecution. Fuller v. Superior Court, 87 Cal. App. 4th 299, 307-308 (2001).denied without prejudice. The incident in question allegedly occurred on June 25, 2011. The civil lawsuit was not filed until June 12, 2013, nearly two years later. The criminal statute of limitations appears to be three years (Penal Code §801), which is about 9 months from now. Plaintiff concurs that an open criminal investigation is "more than sufficient to have a valid 5th amendment privilege to assert." (See Plaintiff's Memorandum of Points and Authorities in Opposition to Motion at 5). A stay will avoid multiple piecemeal motions which are virtually certain to occur given the allegations in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
